title: Rosehip face serum
subtitle: A simple two-oil facial serum with rosehip and rose geranium
excerpt: A facial serum made from rosehip seed oil and jojoba oil, scented at 2% with rose geranium essential oil. Rosehip seed oil is high in linoleic acid and is one of the most researched plant oils for skin. Fills three 30 ml dropper bottles. Use within 6 months.
sourceNotes: Tisserand and Young, Essential Oil Safety (2nd ed., 2014): rose geranium use rate for facial products 0.2-2%. Dobos et al., Efficacy of rosehip oil in treatment of facial scars (Journal of Cosmetic Dermatology, 2012): rosehip oil and skin barrier.

recipe.yieldDescription: 3 x 30 ml dropper bottles (~90 g total)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by the number of bottles needed; the ratio stays the same.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make in small batches to stay within the 6-month rosehip oil shelf life. Refrigerate after opening.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months from making, limited by the oxidation timeline of rosehip seed oil. Refrigerate all bottles after opening. Discard if the serum smells metallic or paint-like — this is rancid rosehip oil.

glossaryTerms.definition: An omega-6 polyunsaturated fatty acid found in high concentrations in rosehip seed oil (around 44 to 55% of its fatty acid profile). Linoleic-acid-rich oils absorb quickly into the skin without leaving a heavy residue and are associated with maintenance of the skin barrier. They are also the most prone to oxidation, which is why rosehip oil has a shorter shelf life than oleic-acid-rich oils like jojoba.

Exposure to air, heat, and light breaks down the polyunsaturated fatty acids in rosehip oil, causing it to turn rancid. Store the finished serum in the amber dropper bottle away from direct light. Refrigerate after opening and use within 6 months.

content.text: A facial serum made from two carrier oils and a small amount of rose geranium essential oil. Rosehip seed oil is high in

and absorbs into the skin faster than most plant oils. Jojoba is technically a liquid wax, not an oil; its fatty acid profile is very similar to the skin's own sebum, which makes it an effective carrier and stabiliser for the more volatile rosehip oil.

: rosehip oil goes rancid faster than most cosmetic oils. Use amber dropper bottles, store away from heat and light, and refrigerate after the first use. A 30 ml bottle used twice daily lasts about 6 weeks, well within the shelf life. Do a patch test on the inner forearm before applying to the face. Rose geranium essential oil has a first-trimester pregnancy caution; omit it if pregnant.

items.prepNote: Cold-pressed rosehip seed oil (Rosa canina). Check the bottle smells fresh, not metallic, before using.
Golden or clear jojoba. Extends the shelf life of the rosehip oil by diluting its polyunsaturated content.
2% of total serum weight. Pelargonium roseum. Within facial-use IFRA guidelines at this dilution.

content.text: Weigh the rosehip oil and jojoba oil directly into a small bowl or jug. Add the rose geranium essential oil and stir briefly. Use the small funnel to fill each amber dropper bottle to within 1 cm of the dropper insert. Seal, shake gently, and label with the making date.

Apply 3 to 4 drops to clean, damp skin at night. Press lightly into the skin rather than rubbing; the oil absorbs within a few minutes. A small amount of residue on the surface in the morning is normal for rosehip oil. Start with a patch test on the inner forearm for 24 hours before applying to the face.

Store sealed bottles in a cool dark place. Refrigerate any bottle once opened. Use within 6 months of the making date. The amber glass protects against light degradation but cannot prevent oxidation entirely once the bottle is opened. If the serum smells metallic, sharp, or paint-like, it has oxidised; discard it.
